title: $1,000 in Bitcoin After Second Halving (2016)
description: What $1,000 invested in Bitcoin on July 10, 2016 — the day after Bitcoin's second block reward halving — would be worth today. The halving reduced block rewards from 25 BTC to 12.5 BTC. Bitcoin was trading at approximately $650. The 2017 bull run to ~$20,000 began from this point.
educational_note: Bitcoin halvings historically precede significant price appreciation 12-18 months later. However, investors had to survive an 83% crash in 2018 before seeing the 2020-2021 bull run. This scenario shows the outcome for a patient holder.
volatility_warning: Bitcoin has experienced multiple 80%+ peak-to-trough crashes. It is one of the most volatile assets in financial history.
